case_title: LESLIE C MCWHIRTER VS. STEVEN A FABBRO

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUN-12-2009 CONTINUED TO JUL-31-2009 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEVEN A. FABBRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS NOT ESTABLISHED FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT. TO THE EXTENT IT ADDRESSES THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, IT WILL BE TREATED AS A MOTION TO STRIKE AND GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS THAT COULD SUPPORT SUCH RELIEF. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Off Calendar Aug-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEVEN FABBRO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Elizabeth A. MacDonald, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415) 922-5939, specially appearing for Defendant and moving party. William S. Cushman, Esq. Attorney at Law, (530) 842-1428 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiff and opposing party. Hearing held and the Court rules as follows: Defendant's Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the punitive damages allegation only, which is stricken. Plaintiff has ten day's leave to amend. Thirty days leave to respond/answer. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Continued To Sep-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEVEN A. FABBRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.
APPEARANCES: William S. Cushman, Esq. 530/842-1428 for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); (Steven A. Fabbro, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven A. Fabbro 391-6850 deft & moving party, requested a CourtCall appearance but failed to appear when the Court called the matter twice).
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS NOT ESTABLISHED FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT. TO THE EXTENT IT ADDRESSES THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, IT WILL BE TREATED AS A MOTION TO STRIKE AND GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS THAT COULD SUPPORT SUCH RELIEF. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
